_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved.

activity_name: Concrete pour — pool shell (poured, not shotcrete)

hazards.title: Concrete burns / dermatitis
hazards.risk: Wet concrete contact causing chemical burns to skin and eyes; cement is highly alkaline

controls.title: Waterproof pants + tall boots if standing in pour
Clean water + soap wash station on site; immediate flush if skin/eye contact

hazards.title: Concrete pump line failure / blowback
hazards.risk: Hose whip or burst projecting concrete at high velocity

controls.title: Pump operator licensed; daily inspection records on site
End-hose restraint where required by operator's procedure
No personnel directly in line with hose end during start-up / line clearing

hazards.title: Mobile plant — concrete truck / pump truck
hazards.risk: Worker on foot struck by reversing truck or pump truck outriggers

controls.title: Spotter for all truck movements; pre-agreed loading/unloading positions
Exclusion zone around outrigger footprint of pump truck during set-up
Traffic management plan for street position of pump truck; council permit if footpath/road affected

hazards.title: Overhead power contact (pump boom)
hazards.risk: Concrete pump boom contacting overhead lines

controls.title: Pump positioned and boom path planned to maintain 6.4m clearance from energised overhead
Electrical spotter if breach risk; asset owner approval if breach unavoidable

hazards.title: Manual handling + vibration (poker vibrator, screeding)
hazards.risk: MSDs from extended use of poker vibrator and screed work

controls.title: Rotate vibrator operators every 20 minutes during long pours

hazards.title: Slips / trips on wet edge
hazards.risk: Wet, slippery pour edges; uneven access platforms

controls.title: Keep edges and access paths clean; squeegee spillage immediately
Safety boots with aggressive tread; treads cleaned during pour

ppe.title: Safety footwear (tall waterproof if in pour)
P2 mask (if dry-mix dust during pump priming)

tasks: Pre-pour confirm: reinforcement signed off, formwork engineer-approved where applicable, plumbing/electrical penetrations boxed, fall lines & blockouts checked.
Weather check — postpone if rain forecast within pour + initial set window (or have wet-weather plan).
Pre-start toolbox with full crew + pump operator + truck driver(s): roles, pour sequence, signals, comms channel.
Council permit and traffic management confirmed if pump truck on street; cones + spotter for street.
Pump truck position set; outriggers down on packers; exclusion zone bunting around outrigger footprint.
Boom positioned; confirm 6.4m clearance from any overhead power.
Set up wash station with clean water and soap before pour starts.
First truck delivers; slump tested; concrete accepted or rejected per spec.
Pour starts at deepest point, working in lifts per engineering spec.
Vibrator operator(s) work the pour progressively; rotate every 20 min.
Screed and finish to spec; protect from rain / hot sun as needed.
Hose / pump line cleared at end of pour per pump operator procedure; no personnel in line.
Clean down: skip for concrete waste; do not wash residue into street, drains, or garden.
Sign-off in Hub when pour complete; record truck dockets, slump test results, ambient temp.
Curing plan in place: water spray / cure compound / wet hessian per spec, duration noted in Hub.
